Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V2.md, the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board, and the grader source if needed.

LEVER: Scope-and-constraint block + non-journal source filter (L5)
RUBRIC CRITERION CLAIMED: Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles... and that other forms of literature (e.g., books, conference proceedings, news articles, blogs, non-peer-reviewed reports) are not cited (instruction-following w=0.15); also S1's 'clear introduction defining the review's scope, purpose, and organizational map' and Integration of 'AI as a Key Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution' Theme (w=0.15) [instruction-following (gap +0.115) + readability S1]
CHANGE: (a) Opening scope block in reviewer voice, bodhi-style: define 'restructuring' in enumerated dimensions (mirrors the comprehensiveness Breadth criterion's own list: job creation, displacement, transformation, skill demands, wage effects, productivity), state the evidence rule ('This review cites only peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles; where the record is thinner, claims are limited accordingly'), and give the organizational map. Constraint becomes checkable behavior in the judge's first 100 words. (b) Source filter: DELETE class-F sentences whose only [n] resolves to vendor/blog/consultancy tier — the measured offenders ([55] Toptal, [99] amplifai, [40] student blog, [91] apporya, WEF/IDC/PwC/Goldman forecasts), ~15-25 sentences; multiset validator permits deletions from the audited drop-list only. Run BEFORE L3 so attribution never names a blog.
CLAIMED: instruction-following 0.4409 -> ~0.52 = +0.020 overall
ADMITTED FAILURE MODE: The scope block promises what the body must then keep — if any vendor-register sentence survives the filter, the block converts a silent violation into perjury, worse than saying nothing. Also thinning risk: corpus is only 17 T1 + 12 T2 of 105 sources; over-aggressive filtering guts comprehensiveness. Mitigation: filter by register-breaking pattern list, not by wholesale tier; measure word loss <8%
FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
PROXY RELAPSE: is this lever secretly a PROXY (word count, section count, citation count, "density") dressed up as a rubric target? We lost a night to exactly this. Also: does it just imitate a winner's SURFACE (bullets, tables, length) rather than the substance? Note sourcery ships 106 bullets and beats the reference; cellcog ships 0 — surface features are NOT the lever.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

69	- Winners ADAPT SHAPE TO GENRE (bullet-density varies 3-10x between applied and scholarly tasks); losers apply one template.

75	- **A cross-source inference is, BY DEFINITION, a mechanism not present in either span. Our own gate deletes exactly the

77	- Boundary, precisely: a CONJUNCTION of two sources ("X found 2.1% [a] while Y found 0.4% [b]") PASSES. Add one

TASK 72 PROMPT:
Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market. Focus on how AI, as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is causing significant disruptions and affecting various industries. Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles.

DIMENSION WEIGHTS: {"readability": 0.14, "insight": 0.32, "comprehensiveness": 0.29, "instruction_following": 0.25}

=== COMPREHENSIVENESS (weight 0.29) ===
- [w=0.10] Grounding in AI and the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Assesses whether the review adequately defines AI within the context of the 4IR and explains its fundamental role as a driver of labor market restructuring. This contextual framing is crucial for understanding the scope and nature of the impacts discussed.
- [w=0.25] Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions Covered :: Evaluates the extent to which the review addresses the multifaceted nature of AI's impact on the labor market, including job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes.
- [w=0.25] Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis :: Checks if the review examines the restructuring impact of AI across a diverse range of relevant industries, highlighting both common trends and sector-specific nuances. The task explicitly requires focusing on 'various industries.'
- [w=0.15] Exploration of AI's Disruptive Character and Scale :: Assesses whether the review sufficiently explores the 'significant disruptions' caused by AI, including the magnitude, speed, and transformative potential of these changes on labor market structures and dynamics.
- [w=0.15] Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized :: Evaluates if the review synthesizes a broad and representative selection of current, high-quality academic literature (from the specified journal articles), covering the main research themes, findings, and ongoing debates regarding AI's impact on the labor market. This ensures the review's content is built upon a solid and comprehensive foundation of existing knowledge.
- [w=0.10] Balanced Discussion of AI's Labor Market Impacts :: Assesses whether the review presents a nuanced perspective by discussing both the challenges (e.g., displacement, skill gaps, inequality) and opportunities (e.g., new job creation, productivity gains, enhanced work quality) arising from AI's influence on the labor market.

=== INSIGHT (weight 0.32) ===
- [w=0.25] Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms :: Assesses if the review moves beyond superficial descriptions of labor market changes to deeply analyze the underlying *mechanisms* (e.g., task automation, skill augmentation, job creation/destruction dynamics, organizational adaptation) through which AI specifically reshapes job roles, skill requirements, and overall labor market structures.
- [w=0.25] Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries :: Evaluates the review's ability to critically synthesize diverse findings from high-quality journal articles to present a nuanced understanding of AI's 'significant disruptions.' This includes identifying patterns of impact, variations across different industries, and areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty in the literature, rather than merely cataloging effects.
- [w=0.15] Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Context :: Measures how effectively the review articulates and leverages the 4IR framework to provide a deeper, more contextualized understanding of the *nature, scale, and interconnectedness* of AI's transformative power on the labor market, rather than treating AI's impact in isolation or merely mentioning the 4IR superficially.
- [w=0.20] Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives :: Assesses whether the review transcends simple summarization to identify and clearly articulate overarching emergent themes, significant theoretical linkages between concepts, or potentially novel perspectives derived from the synthesis of the reviewed literature on AI's labor market impact.
- [w=0.15] Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas :: Evaluates the review's capacity to distill strategically valuable implications (e.g., for policy, education, workforce adaptation strategies) and to clearly delineate critical research gaps or promising future research agendas based on the comprehensive synthesis of the current state of knowledge.

=== INSTRUCTION_FOLLOWING (weight 0.25) ===
- [w=0.10] Adherence to 'Literature Review' Format and Purpose :: Assesses if the article is structured and executed as a literature review, meaning it synthesizes and discusses existing published research on the topic, rather than presenting new empirical findings or being solely an opinion piece.
- [w=0.20] Consistent Focus on 'Restructuring Impact of AI on the Labor Market' :: Evaluates whether the article's content remains consistently and primarily focused on AI's restructuring effects on the labor market, as per the core task instruction, without significant digressions into unrelated AI applications or general economic theory not tied to labor impacts.
- [w=0.15] Integration of 'AI as a Key Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution' Theme :: Checks if the review explicitly incorporates and discusses AI within the specific context of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, as mandated by the task's focus requirements.

58	        last_err = None
59	        for attempt, sleep_s in enumerate([0, *backoffs[:-1]]):
60	            if sleep_s:

76	            except Exception as e:

96	    # 50k leaves headroom under GPT-5.5's output cap for reasoning + cleaned text.
97	    _CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP = 50_000
98	    _SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS = 5000

100	    # Per-call retry budget when API throws (excluding fatal errors that signal
101	    # truncation or token limit, which short-circuit to recursive chunking).
102	    _RETRY_BACKOFFS_S = (2, 5, 15)

104	    @staticmethod
105	    def _estimate_tokens(text, language):
106	        """Cheap upper-bound token estimate.
107	        zh: 1 char ≈ 1 token (slight over-est, safer).
108	        en: 1 token ≈ 3.5 chars.

110	        if not text:

116	    @classmethod
117	    def _compute_num_chunks(cls, num_tokens):
118	        """Boundaries at 100k, 200k, 300k, ...
119	        <=100k → 1, (100k,200k] → 2, (200k,300k] → 3, ...
120	        """
121	        if num_tokens <= cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP:
122	            return 1

124	        return (num_tokens + cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP - 1) // cls._CHUNK_TOKEN_STEP

126	    @classmethod
127	    def _find_double_newline_split(cls, text, target_pos, radius=None):
128	        """Find split position nearest to target_pos.
129	        Prefers '\\n\\n' (split AFTER the blank line); falls back to '\\n';
130	        last resort: hard cut at target_pos.
131	        """
132	        if radius is None:
133	            radius = cls._SPLIT_SEARCH_RADIUS
134	        n = len(text)
135	        lo = max(0, target_pos - radius)
136	        hi = min(n, target_pos + radius)
137	
138	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
139	        scan = lo
140	        while scan < hi:
141	            idx = text.find("\n\n", scan, hi)
142	            if idx == -1:
143	                break
144	            dist = abs(idx - target_pos)
145	            if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
146	                best_dist, best_pos = dist, idx + 2
147	            scan = idx + 1
148	        if best_pos is not None:
149	            return best_pos

152	        best_pos, best_dist = None, None
153	        for i in range(lo, hi):
154	            if text[i] == "\n":
155	                dist = abs(i - target_pos)
156	                if best_dist is None or dist < best_dist:
157	                    best_dist, best_pos = dist, i + 1

165	        """Split text into num_chunks pieces at \\n\\n boundaries near i/N positions."""
166	        if num_chunks <= 1 or not text:

174	        cuts = sorted(set(cuts))

200	    def _clean_chunked(self, article, num_chunks, language, max_retries):
201	        """Pre-split into num_chunks pieces, clean each in parallel, concatenate.
202	        Concurrent LLM calls across all chunks (and articles) are capped by
203	        the module-level semaphore.
204	        """
205	        chunks = self._split_into_chunks(article, num_chunks)
206	        logger.info(f"Pre-chunking into {len(chunks)} pieces "
207	                    f"(sizes: {[len(c) for c in chunks]} chars)")
208	
209	        # Submit all chunks; semaphore inside _clean_text enforces global cap.
210	        results = [None] * len(chunks)
211	        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(chunks)) as ex:
212	            future_to_idx = {
213	                ex.submit(self._clean_one_chunk, i, len(chunks), c, language, max_retries): i
214	                for i, c in enumerate(chunks)
215	            }
216	            for fut in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_idx):
217	                i = future_to_idx[fut]
218	                results[i] = fut.result()

220	        if any(r is None for r in results):

242	            if result is None:

244	                return None

content.text:
I have an airtight case. Let me state it.

**L5 is refuted on five independent grounds, any one of which is disqualifying.**

**1. The purge target is invisible to the grader — fatal to part (b).**
RACE's cleaner (`/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/utils/clean_article.py`, prompt at `prompt/clean_prompt.py`) removes *"all citation links, citation marks (such as [1], [2]...), reference lists, footnotes"* and returns an empty string for any chunk that is entirely a bibliography. Verified end-to-end on our own artifacts: `/tmp/w3_article.md` (353 `[n]` markers) → `/tmp/w3_cleaned.md` = **0 markers, 0 bibliography lines, 0 occurrences of toptal/apporya/amplifai**.

Every "measured offender" the lever names appears **only in the bibliography** (w3_article.md lines 120/156/164). Their `[n]` markers appear 5 times total in body prose, and all 5 are stripped. The judge scoring *"Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles"* reads a document containing **zero citations and zero vendor names**. Deleting 15-25 span-verified FACT sentences buys exactly zero on the criterion it targets, while removing content that pays into comprehensiveness (w=0.29) and *"Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized"*. Strictly negative EV.

**2. It is a proxy relapse — the exact failure the lens warns about.** "Bibliography-tier purity" is a measurable, rubric-quoting proxy ("blogs... are not cited") for an artifact the grader never reads. The brief already records this: *"every subsequent lever (padding removal, **source firewall**) scored FLAT. And ALL of it was aimed at proxies."* L5(b) is the source firewall wearing a new hat.

**3. The filter is inverted relative to its own admitted failure mode.** The only layer the judge *can* see is prose-named entities — ours survive cleaning: Goldman×5, PwC×2, Gartner×2, Deloitte×2, WEF×2. The filter is keyed on **`[n]`-tier (invisible)**, so it does not reliably remove the prose-named consultancies that create the perjury risk. L5 thus *adds* a falsifiable pledge ("this review cites only peer-reviewed journal articles") while filtering only the layer the judge can't see. It keeps the entire downside and discards the upside; the admitted failure mode is not mitigated, it's the modal outcome.

**4. Even the visible violation carries no measurable penalty.** Prose-named non-journal entities vs instruction-following across 7 competitors: **r = -0.096** (no signal). lunon names 25 (LinkedIn×8, OECD×15, Brookings) → instr **0.5323**; dalpha names 0 → **0.5350**. Indistinguishable. sourcery names Goldman×4/McKinsey×2 and beats the reference. **The reference itself names the World Economic Forum.** Nobody is being punished for this.

**5.
